entries.riskLabel: Neutral · range mode — deleveraging completing; awaiting BTC $74.2k reclaim + PCE
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Modest weekend follow-through to Friday's bounce: BTC +0.6% to $73.6k (range $72.4–74.2k), ETH leading +1.0%, gold incremental bid +0.5%. Big positioning cleanup across alts (SOL OI −8.6%, XRP OI −10%) — healthy deleveraging reset, not euphoric long-stacking. Friday's MSCI rebalance is behind us; equities closed for the weekend. No confirmed trend repair until BTC reclaims $74.2k; PCE remains the swing event.

entries.riskLabel: Neutral, leaning constructive — short-covering bounce; awaiting BTC $74k reclaim + PCE
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Bear-case from yesterday is partially unwinding: BTC bouncing +0.7% to $73.7k (held the $72k support), ETH leading +1.5%, gold ripping +2.9% on safe-haven flows. OI down across crypto while price up = clean short-covering bounce. Today is MSCI rebalance day — IDX peak forced selling live now.

entries.riskLabel: Defensive bias — crypto broke down despite falling yields; awaiting PCE
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: Crypto broke down: BTC lost the $74.2k 'bear-trigger' from yesterday's note → $72,900 (−2.8%), ETH worse at $1,987 (−3.4%). The dovish-Fed + Iran de-escalation backdrop (10Y ~4.48%) hasn't been enough — risk-off is leaking out via crypto first. US equities still near records, gold catching a slight safe-haven bid. PCE remains the swing event.

entries.riskLabel: Neutral, tilting constructive — the rates headwind is reversing
entries.bottomLineExcerpt: The regime is turning: a newly sworn-in, easing-leaning Fed (Warsh) + US–Iran de-escalation is pulling yields down (10Y ~4.48%, a 2-week low) and pricing out the hikes that hammered crypto. Crypto is basing, US equities at records. Offsets: mainland-China tech/semi selling, Indonesia's 29 May MSCI outflow cliff, and the PCE inflation print as the swing event.
